name: Route of Charms (PR9 TVD)
name_original: Rota da Serra do Socorro — percurso circular
description: Hike the moderate 7.3-mile Serra do Socorro circular route, featuring panoramic views and historical landmarks like Forte da Archeira.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Turcifal offer a blend of natural beauty and historical significance within the Ribatejo region of Portugal. The area features diverse landscapes, including hills, valleys, and both agricultural and wooded lands, particularly around the Serra do Socorro. This region is part of a protected landscape, characterized by a transition between Mediterranean and Atlantic climates. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from river floodplains to areas with cork oak groves. Best easy hiking trails around Turcifal The most popular easy hiking route is Ribeirinho (Azul) trail in Tapada de Mafra, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 10 minutes to complete. This route offers gentle gradients through the diverse natural habitat of Tapada Nacional de Mafra. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rio Sizandro – Bordinheira loop from Bordinheira, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This trail follows the Rio Sizandro, providing views of the river and surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Almeirinhos, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Turcifal is defined by rolling hills, agricultural fields, and wooded areas, with trails that often feature historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, focusing on accessible and enjoyable easy hiking trails. The routes in Turcifal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 390 hikers have used komoot to explore Turcifal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Turcifal?
A: There are 10 easy hiking trails specifically curated for the Turcifal area. In total, komoot offers 38 routes in the region, catering to various difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on easy hikes near Turcifal?
A: Easy hikes around Turcifal offer a diverse mix of landscapes, including rolling hills, agricultural fields, and wooded areas. You'll find trails traversing river floodplains, cork oak groves, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapada Nacional de Mafra&lt;/a&gt;, which features a rich natural habitat.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes in Turcifal?
A: Yes, many easy trails in the Turcifal area are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32168143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Sizandro – Bordinheira loop from Bordinheira&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.2 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32169430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Almeirinhos&lt;/a&gt; covers 6.2 km through varied landscapes.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I see on easy trails?
A: Many easy trails in the region feature historical landmarks and offer scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32205123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Forte da Archeira&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forte da Archeira&lt;/a&gt;. The broader Serra do Socorro area, part of the Lines of Torres Vedras, also offers panoramic views from near the Ermida de Nossa Senhora do Socorro.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Turcifal suitable for families?
A: Yes, the easy hiking trails around Turcifal are generally suitable for families due to their gentle gradients and manageable distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380887272/ribeirinho-azul-trail-in-tapada-de-mafra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeirinho (Azul) trail in Tapada de Mafra&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a short 4.4 km route perfect for a family outing.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Turcifal?
A: The easy trails in Turcifal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical interest.

Q: Are there any easy coastal walks near Turcifal?
A: While Turcifal itself is inland, the wider region offers easy coastal walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32159806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz do Arelho Coastal Scenery loop from São Pedro da Cadeira&lt;/a&gt; provides an easy 6.8 km route with beautiful coastal views.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Turcifal?
A: The Ribatejo region, where Turcifal is located, experiences a transition between Mediterranean and Atlantic climates, characterized by mild temperatures. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for hiking, avoiding the hotter summer months and potential winter rains, making them ideal for easy walks.

Q: Are there any specific eco-paths or nature trails in the area?
A: Yes, the Eco Caminho Turcifal / Serra do Socorro is a significant eco-path in the region. It's about 5.7 km long and passes by landmarks like Quinta do Manjapão and Capela da Cadriceira, featuring bird observatories and a picnic park. You can find more information about this path on &lt;a href="https://www.visitetorresvedras.pt/locais/explorar/pedalar/eco-caminho-da-serra-do-socorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitetorresvedras.pt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails around Turcifal?
A: Many outdoor areas in Portugal, including natural parks and trails, are generally dog-friendly, especially on leash. However, specific rules can vary by location, particularly in protected areas like Tapada Nacional de Mafra. It's always advisable to check local signage or park regulations for the specific trail you plan to hike.

Q: What is the typical duration for an easy hike in Turcifal?
A: Easy hikes in Turcifal typically range from 1 to 2 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380887272/ribeirinho-azul-trail-in-tapada-de-mafra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeirinho (Azul) trail&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour 10 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32169430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Almeirinhos&lt;/a&gt; is often completed in about 2 hours.
tips: Forte da Archeira, part of the Lines of Towers defensive north of Lisbon during the Peninsular War, is a military fortification located at an altitude of 345 m. It was part of the 1st Defensive Line with the forts of Catefica and Feiteira. Despite not having gunboats, it has traces of a magazine. It forms part of the Lines of Torres, a system of 152 fortifications stretching over 80 km, built between 1809 and 1812 to prevent the advance of Napoleonic troops.
Very beautiful chapel with simple architecture in the center of the village of Furadouro. Largo da Capela - and its small car park - is an ideal point for exploring the Archeira mountain range.

Located at the top of the mountain, the Hermitage of Nossa Senhora do Socorro, originally a mosque, was converted into a Catholic temple by D. Afonso Henriques and rebuilt during the reign of D. Manuel I. It offers panoramic views and displays Manueline stones inside.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Route of Serra do Socorro?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data suggests this circular hiking route takes approximately 3 hours and 44 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace, stops for photos, and breaks.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hiking route?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies the 'Route of Serra do Socorro — circular route' as moderate. While the area also features more challenging trail running routes with significant elevation gain, this specific hiking trail is designed to be accessible for those with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse terrain, including sections of road, designated hiking paths, footpaths, and streets. For those undertaking the more challenging versions in the area, surfaces can also include asphalt, paved, gravel, and unpaved sections. Generally, you'll encounter a mix of surfaces as you traverse the landscape.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Route of Serra do Socorro?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. You'll encounter the Forte da Archeira, a military fortification that was part of the Lines of Torres Vedras. Other notable sites include the Chapel of Our Lady of the Guide and the Ermida de Nossa Senhora do Socorro, which offers expansive panoramic views from the mountain's summit.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts and ends at the Interpretative Center of PPLSSA in Cadriceira. While specific parking details aren't provided, starting points for trails in protected landscapes often have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Route of Serra do Socorro dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Paisagem Protegida Local das Serras do Socorro e Archeira. While many trails in Portugal are dog-friendly, regulations within protected landscapes can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific rules regarding dogs in this particular protected area should be verified locally or on official park websites.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The 'Route of Serra do Socorro — circular route' is located within the Paisagem Protegida Local das Serras do Socorro e Archeira. Generally, local protected landscapes in Portugal do not require specific hiking permits or charge entrance fees for access to trails. However, it's always a good practice to check the official website of the protected landscape (e.g., visitetorresvedras.pt) for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so an early morning start is recommended if hiking during warmer months. Winters are generally mild but can bring rain.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route significantly overlaps with other official trails. Approximately 92% of the route follows sections of the GR30 - Grande Rota Linhas de Torres Vedras and the Rota dos Encantos. A smaller portion, about 11%, also coincides with the Estrada Militar.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail traverses the Local Protected Landscape of Serras do Socorro and Archeira, known for its rich natural and landscape heritage. The Hermitage of Nossa Senhora do Socorro, situated at the mountain's summit, is a key scenic point offering expansive panoramic vistas of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail typically starts and ends at the Interpretative Center of PPLSSA in Cadriceira, specific public transport options directly to this trailhead are not detailed. It's advisable to research local bus services or regional transport links to Cadriceira or nearby towns and then consider a short taxi ride or walk to the starting point.
